description_long: This is a large, bulbous ceramic vase with a rounded body that tapers toward a narrow base and a slightly flared rim. The surface is matte and textured, with a natural beige coloration and subtle variations in tone, giving it an earthy, handmade appearance. Three raised, broken protrusions are visible on one side, possibly remnants of handles or decorative elements, while the opposite side is smooth and unadorned. The rim is thick and slightly uneven, with a small chip visible, suggesting some age or wear. Two incised horizontal bands encircle the upper portion of the vase, adding a simple decorative touch. The overall form and finish evoke traditional pottery often found in Mediterranean or Middle Eastern urban settings, suitable for outdoor or indoor display.
description: A large, beige ceramic vase with a rounded body, flared rim, and subtle incised bands, featuring broken protrusions on one side and a matte, textured finish.
description_view_0: The vase is seen with its smooth side forward, showing the rounded body, incised bands, and a thick rim with a visible chip.
description_view_1: This view highlights the vase's unadorned side, with the incised bands and rim chip more clearly visible, and no protrusions present.
description_view_2: Three broken protrusions are visible on the right side of the vase, along with the incised bands and the overall bulbous shape.
description_view_3: The broken protrusions are most prominent in this view, running vertically along the upper right side, with the rim and bands also visible.
